nodes.context: Dissident Irish republican paramilitary group aiming for a united Ireland, formed in 1997 after a split from the Provisional IRA.
Irish republican paramilitary group that called a ceasefire in 1997, leading to a split that formed the Real IRA.
New entity formed in July 2012 from the merger of the Real IRA, Republican Action Against Drugs, and other small republican militant groups.
Small republican militant group that merged with the Real IRA in July 2012 to form the New IRA.
Political wing of the Real IRA, founded by Bernadette Sands McKevitt.
Dissident Irish republican paramilitary group aiming for a United Ireland, formed in 1997 after a split from the Provisional IRA.
Irish republican paramilitary group that split in 1997, leading to the formation of the Real IRA.
Another Provisional IRA splinter group founded in 1986.
Small republican militant group that merged with the Real IRA in 2012.
Political wing of the Real IRA.
Unregistered political party and political wing of the Real IRA.

edges.evidence_quote: It was formed in 1997 following a split in the Provisional IRA by dissident members, who rejected the IRA's ceasefire that year.
edges.context: The Real IRA split from the Provisional IRA in 1997.

edges.evidence_quote: In July 2012, it was reported that Republican Action Against Drugs (RAAD) and other small republican militant groups were merging with the Real IRA. This new entity was named the New IRA by the media
edges.context: The Real IRA merged with RAAD and other groups to form the New IRA in July 2012.

edges.evidence_quote: In July 2012, it was reported that Republican Action Against Drugs (RAAD) and other small republican militant groups were merging with the Real IRA.
edges.context: The Real IRA merged with RAAD in July 2012.

edges.evidence_quote: Bernadette Sands McKevitt, sister of hunger striker Bobby Sands and a founder of the RIRA's political wing, the 32 County Sovereignty Movement
edges.context: The 32 County Sovereignty Movement is the political wing of the Real IRA.

edges.evidence_quote: It was formed in 1997 following a split in the Provisional IRA by dissident members, who rejected the IRA's ceasefire that year.
edges.context: The Real IRA split from the Provisional IRA in 1997.

edges.evidence_quote: The Provisional IRA has been hostile to the RIRA and issued threats to RIRA members, and in October 2000 was alleged to be responsible for the fatal shooting of Belfast RIRA member Joe O'Connor according to O'Connor's family and 32 County Sovereignty Movement member Marian Price.
edges.context: The Provisional IRA has been hostile to the Real IRA, issuing threats and allegedly killing a member.

edges.evidence_quote: The RIRA is distinct from the Continuity IRA, another Provisional IRA splinter group founded in 1986, although the two groups have been known to co-operate at a local level.
edges.context: The Real IRA and Continuity IRA have cooperated at a local level.

edges.evidence_quote: On 26 July 2012, it was reported that Republican Action Against Drugs (RAAD) and other small republican militant groups were merging with the Real IRA.
edges.context: RAAD merged with the Real IRA in 2012.

edges.evidence_quote: The RIRA also has political wings: the 32 County Sovereignty Movement (formerly the 32 County Sovereignty Committee), led by Francis Mackey, and unregistered political party Saoradh, led by Brian Kenna.
edges.context: The 32 County Sovereignty Movement is a political wing of the Real IRA.

edges.evidence_quote: The RIRA also has political wings: the 32 County Sovereignty Movement (formerly the 32 County Sovereignty Committee), led by Francis Mackey, and unregistered political party Saoradh, led by Brian Kenna.
edges.context: Saoradh is a political wing of the Real IRA.
